text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. Readable code is easy to understand not only for yourself but also for others who may work on it later. One important practice is to use clear naming conventions for variables and functions; a name like "calculateTotal" is much better than "calc". Keep your functions small and focused on a single task. This makes your code easier to test and debug. Overall, writing clean code leads to better maintainability, as it allows developers to make changes and fix bugs quickly without breaking other parts of the program.
